primary.note: Emerging keyword with 30/mo baseline. Will spike massively as FIFA World Cup 2026 approaches (June 2026). Massive informational keywords in cluster drive total volume.

competitor_analysis.serp_overview: SERP for 'world cup groups betting' is underdeveloped as an emerging keyword pre-tournament. High-volume informational terms like 'world cup groups' (30K/mo) and 'world cup draw' (60K/mo) are dominated by FIFA.com, Wikipedia, and major sports news outlets. Betting-specific group stage content is sparse, creating a first-mover advantage for a comprehensive group-by-group betting guide.
key_insights: Informational queries 'world cup groups' (30K) and 'world cup draw' (60K) dwarf betting-specific terms but drive massive related traffic
48-team expanded format for 2026 creates 12 groups instead of 8, increasing content depth opportunity
Three host nations (USA, Mexico, Canada) ensure sustained domestic search interest
Group of death narratives always generate high engagement and social sharing
Low KD on betting-specific terms (KD 4-40) means easy ranking opportunities before volume spikes

content_gaps: Complete 12-group listing with all 48 teams and betting odds per group
Group of death analysis with betting angle and value picks
Per-group winner odds comparison across sportsbooks
Group stage-specific bet type guide (group winner, advancement, match results)
Interactive group standings tracker with live updates
Strategy section for parlaying group stage outcomes
competitor_analysis.competitive_advantage: First comprehensive group-by-group World Cup 2026 betting guide combining complete group listings, per-group odds, group of death analysis, and sportsbook comparison. The expanded 48-team format with 12 groups is new territory, so no competitor has established content for this structure.

brand_selection.rationale: Primary keyword 'world cup groups betting' at 30/mo (emerging, pre-spike) qualifies for 5 brands per the sub-1,000/mo rule. Selected brands with strongest soccer/international betting markets.
